action.hypothesis: After eight straight no-improve rounds and with gated already mapped at K=32/64/128, the least-bad informative move is a fundamentally different architecture that changes the input geometry rather than doing another local sweep. A `whitened_topk` proxy run at K=128 under the current strongest gated recipe can test whether a learned input preconditioner makes the activation space easier to sparsify and opens a new quality frontier without coupling in optimizer or K changes.
action.summary: Run a `whitened_topk` proxy comparison at K=128, keeping the current best signum/EF=16/aux-free recipe fixed and changing only architecture.
action.self_review: This satisfies the stagnation policy by trying a new architecture family instead of another minor gated tweak. Risk: the current `whitened_topk` implementation is only a shallow learned preconditioner and may add compute without enough representational benefit, but proxy is the correct tier for that read.
notes_to_memory: Severe stagnation with gated already explored at K=32/64/128 triggered a new-family architecture probe rather than more local tuning.
This round tests input-space preconditioning as the main variable via `whitened_topk`, holding the current best optimization recipe fixed.
next_hypotheses: If `whitened_topk` is materially better than recent non-gated alternatives, keep incubating it with a family-specific stabilization pass before any full request.
If `whitened_topk` is clearly uncompetitive, pivot to another genuinely different architecture mechanism rather than returning to minor gated parameter sweeps.
If quality looks promising but runtime is abnormally slow, follow with an `edit_perf_code` round instead of discarding the family on architecture grounds.
